Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
John K. Cotter 
Attorney at Law 
Administration 
322·5660 
800 H Street, Suite 201 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Cotter: 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
May 23, 1983 
Enforcement 
3224«1 
Statements of Economic: Int_st 
322~444 
Re: 
Your Letter Requesting Advice 
Our No. A-83-128 
Your letter requests advice based upon generalized facts on 
behalf of an unnamed public official. 
Pursuant to Government 
Code Section 83114(b) , the Commission staff will provide written 
advice, upon receipt of all the material facts, to any public 
official with duties or responsibilities under the Political 
Reform Act.ll 
Pursuant to Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. 
Code Section 18329 (b) (1) 1 the request by a public official for 
such advice must provide the identity of the official requesting 
advice. 
The request itself may come from an auhorized 
representative of the official (such as yourself). 
The immunity 
provided by Section 83114(b) attaches to the official who is 
thus named. 
I have attempted unsuccessfully to reach you by 
phone to ascertain your client's name and additional, material 
facts. 
Generally, the actions which you describe could lead to 
conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act. 
In 
addition, the sketchy facts you provide suggest that your client 
may wish to seek the opinion of Legislative Counsel as to the 
possible application of tne Legislative Code of Ethics, 
Government Code Sections 8920, et ~. 
II Government Code Sections 81000-91014. 


John K. cotter 
May 23, 1983 
Page 2 
If you desire further assistance please advise us and 
provide the necessary information. 
I may be reached at 
(916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Sincerely,

/f 
/ 
)

Counsel 
Legal Division

/"'---


LAW OFFICES 
OF 
.JOH~ K. COT'rEH 
800 H STREET. SUITE 201 
SACRAMENTO. CALIFORNIA 95814 
TELEPHONE 
916-447-1046 
May 10, 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
POB 807 
Sacramento, CA 95807 
Dear Commission Hembers: 
On behalf of a client, I request opinions on the 
following questions: 
May a member of the Capitol or District Office staff 
of a member of the Legislature, or any other employee of the 
State of California, do any of the following: 
(1) 
With respect to an organization that lobbies 
members of the Legislature: 
(a) 
Lobby a member of the Legislature; 
(b) 
Advise such an organization as to 
lobbying techniques or as to information 
gained through his employment, either 
before such an organization is formed, 
or after such an organization is formed, 
and engaged in lobbying; 
(c) 
Be a shareholder in such an organization; 
(d) 
Invest money in such an organization; 
(e) 
Receive dividends or like payments (i.e, 
not related to any services rendered) 
from such an organization? 
(2) 
Share ownership of, participate with, or conduct 
business in, a non-conflicting (as defined by 
law in this area) business enterprise (e.g., 
international trade, sales programs, economic 
development programs for local government, media 
relations, and computer program management)? 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. If I can 
answer any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me. 
JKC:er 
cc: Client 




P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Administration 
(916) 322·5662 
John K. Cotter 
Attorney at Law 
800 H Street, Suite 201 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Cotter: 
322-5660 
Executive/legal 
32:?-5901 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
September 16, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, 
Our No. A-83-128 
Pursuant to our recently renewed discussions on this matter, 
we have mutually agreed that it is premature for your client to 
seek specific, written advice at this time. 
I have orally 
provided you with general advice on the possible application of 
the Political Reform Act to your client's circumstances and I 
enclose herewith some additional materials which you may find of 
assistance in determining when your client should seek our 
advice. 
Should you have further questions please do not hesitate to 
call me at (916) 322-5901. 
REL :plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
~t.... 
>J 
Robert E. Leldigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question: May a member of the Capitol or District Office staff of a member of the Legislature, or any other employee of the State of California: (1) With respect to an organization that lobbies members of the Legislature: (a) Lobby a member of the Legislature; (b) Advise such an organization as to lobbying techniques or as to information gained through his employment; (c) Be a shareholder in such an organization; (d) Invest money in such an organization; (e) Receive dividends or like payments from such an organization? (2) Share ownership of, participate with, or conduct business in, a non-conflicting business enterprise?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether legislative staff and state employees may engage in various activities related to lobbying organizations and non-conflicting business enterprises, including shareholding, investment, and receipt of dividends.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined it was premature for written specific advice at that time and provided only general oral guidance, noting that the actions described could lead to conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act and recommending consultation with Legislative Counsel regarding the Legislative Code of Ethics.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (May 10, 1983) and two FPPC response letters (May 23 and September 16, 1983). The initial declination stated the client's identity was required for immunity under Gov. Code § 83114(b). The final response indicates oral advice was provided and written advice deferred as premature.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a member of the Capitol or District Office staff of a member of the Legislature, or any other employee of the State of California: (1) With respect to an organization that lobbies members of the Legislature: (a) Lobby a member of the Legislature; (b) Advise such an organization as to lobbying techniques or as to information gained through his employment; (c) Be a shareholder in such an organization; (d) Invest money in such an organization; (e) Receive dividends or like payments from such an organization? (2) Share ownership of, participate with, or conduct business in, a non-conflicting business enterprise?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined it was premature for written specific advice at that time and provided only general oral guidance, noting that the actions described could lead to conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act and recommending consultation with Legislative Counsel regarding the Legislative Code of Ethics.

embedding.summary: An attorney requested FPPC advice on behalf of an unnamed public official regarding whether state employees and legislative staff may engage in lobbying activities and invest in related organizations. The FPPC initially declined to provide written advice without the official's identity and complete factual details, later agreeing with the requestor that specific written advice was premature and providing only general oral guidance.
